P2P Insights
Platforms
Bondora Mintos

Mintos Review (2026)

Loans, bonds, real estate, ETFs, Smart Cash, crypto, fees & risks

What Is Mintos?

Mintos is one of the most established P2P and multi-asset investing platforms in Europe. It started with loan investing, but today it is much broader: loans, bonds, real estate, ETFs, Smart Cash, and crypto ETPs are all available from one account.

Stocks are expected to be added as another product area, but the core reason to use Mintos today is still the same: it gives investors more choice and more control than simple one-product P2P platforms.

How Mintos Works

Before investing, you verify your account, complete the suitability assessment, deposit cash, and then choose how you want the money to work.

  1. Deposit funds into your available cash balance.
  2. Pick a product: loans, bonds, ETFs, real estate, Smart Cash, or crypto ETPs.
  3. Invest manually, use ready-made portfolios, or build custom automation.
  4. Reinvest returns, collect cash, cash out eligible products, or sell on the Secondary Market.

This is the big difference versus Bondora Go & Grow. On Mintos, you can buy specific loan-backed securities, filter by many criteria, use Core Loans, or create Custom Loans portfolios that invest based on rules you choose.

Mintos vs Bondora: More Control, More Complexity

Bondora Go & Grow is built around simplicity. You deposit money, Bondora allocates it, and you mostly leave it alone.

Mintos is different. It can be passive if you use automated portfolios, but it also rewards investors who want to understand loan originators, risk scores, maturities, interest rates, currencies, and product-specific liquidity.

Returns: Higher Potential, but Product-Dependent

Mintos does not have one simple return number because it is not one simple product. Returns depend heavily on the asset class and the risks you choose.

  • Loans show a current average interest rate of 10.78%.
  • Loans have produced an average annual net return of 8.8% since 2015.
  • Bonds show an 8.75% current average yield.
  • Smart Cash currently offers up to 2.0% interest.

Those numbers are useful reference points, not guarantees. Your actual return can be higher or lower depending on the loans, bonds, portfolios, market conditions, fees, and defaults you experience.

Liquidity: Fast Cash Movement, but Not Instant for Everything

Mintos is good at moving available cash, but invested money should not be treated like a bank balance.

  • Bank transfer deposits are free.
  • Withdrawals from available cash are free.
  • EUR withdrawals to SEPA Instant accounts usually take about 30 minutes.
  • Other withdrawals usually take 1-2 business days.
  • Invested products depend on cash-out availability, maturity, or Secondary Market demand.

Important: Mintos can feel liquid when you are holding available cash, but loans, bonds, real estate, ETFs, Smart Cash, and crypto all have their own market and settlement conditions.

Fees: Mostly Low, but Worth Understanding

Mintos is transparent about fees, and many basic actions are free. Bank transfers and withdrawals are free, and manual loan, bond, ETF, and real estate investing do not carry Mintos investing fees.

The fees to watch:

  • Core Loans portfolios: 0.39% per year.
  • Custom Loans portfolios: 0.29% per year.
  • Smart Cash: 0.19% per year.
  • Crypto ETPs: 0.49% per transaction, minimum EUR 0.99.
  • Card, Apple Pay, and Google Pay deposits: 2%.
  • Currency exchange: from 0.50%.
  • Secondary Market selling: 0.85%.
  • Inactivity fee: EUR 4.90 per month if the account qualifies as inactive.

Risk: What You Should Actually Understand

Mintos is reputable and regulated, but that does not make it risk free. The platform gives you access to real investment products, and real investment products can lose money.

Key risks:

  • Platform risk: you rely on Mintos to operate correctly.
  • Loan originator risk: lending companies can run into trouble.
  • Late and defaulted loans can reduce cash flow and returns.
  • Liquidity depends on the product, market demand, and settlement timing.
  • Secondary Market exits are not guaranteed.
  • Bonds, real estate, ETFs, Smart Cash, and crypto each have product-specific risks.
  • Crypto ETPs can be highly volatile.

Investor compensation can protect against certain failures by the investment firm, but it does not protect you from normal investment losses, poor performance, defaults, or market price changes.

Who Mintos Is Best For

Mintos makes sense if you:

  • Want broader diversification than a single P2P product.
  • Want access to loans, bonds, real estate, ETFs, cash-like products, and crypto ETPs.
  • Like choosing specific loans or building your own automated rules.
  • Are willing to understand originators, risk scores, maturities, and fees.
  • Want higher-yield alternatives and accept the extra risk that comes with them.

Who Should Avoid Mintos

You should skip Mintos if you:

  • Want the simplest possible P2P experience.
  • Expect guaranteed returns or guaranteed instant liquidity.
  • Do not want to compare products, risk levels, or lending companies.
  • Need your invested money to behave like a savings account.

Pros & Cons Summary

Pros

  • Large, reputable European investing platform.
  • Broad product range from one account.
  • Manual and automated loan investing.
  • Higher return potential than simpler P2P products.
  • Free bank transfers and withdrawals.
  • Useful tools for diversification and filtering.

Cons

  • More complex than Bondora Go & Grow.
  • Returns vary significantly by product and strategy.
  • Loan originator and default risk still matter.
  • Invested money is not always instantly liquid.
  • Some fees can matter if you use automation, card deposits, FX, or the Secondary Market.
  • Crypto ETPs add high volatility.

Final Verdict

Mintos is one of the strongest options in Europe if you want a broad, flexible investing platform rather than a simple P2P product.

If you want the easiest possible hands-off experience, Bondora is still simpler. If you want more choice, more control, and access to a wider range of income-producing assets, Mintos is the more powerful platform, as long as you respect the risks.

Start Investing on Mintos

If you are ready to test Mintos, start small, compare the available products, and build gradually as you understand the platform.

Visit Mintos

Product availability, terms, and eligibility can vary by country.
